headline: Mag 7 shrinks $2.3T but chipmakers still backed
detail: Mag 7 valuation pressure from AI spending concerns is a direct headwind to sentiment across your entire book. However, the continued investor backing for chipmakers is a partial offset - it helps SMH and SOXL hold bids. The tension between these two forces is exactly why your leveraged chip names are fragile right now.
rec: No action yet - watch whether SMH holds its current level. If it breaks toward $580, SOXL's $200 stop becomes the urgent line; no reason to add or trim today purely on this headline.

headline: AI trade could power higher in H2 - What it would take
detail: If the AI trade re-accelerates in H2, your concentrated tech/chip book is positioned to benefit most - CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and QQQ all move on this theme. But 'could' and 'wouldn't take much' are speculative, not confirmed catalyst language. Do not size up margin on hope.
rec: No new buys on this alone - it supports holding CRWD and letting the scanner's grade-A long setup play out naturally if price holds 741-742, but it is not a strong enough reason to add fresh margin exposure today.

headline: Digital Realty drops 5% on Blackstone data center deal
detail: Data center infrastructure stress can bleed into AI/cloud sentiment, which touches your names indirectly - particularly anything cloud-adjacent like CRWD. A 5% drop in a data center name on a deal that looks dilutive or risky signals the market is scrutinizing AI infrastructure spending quality, not just quantity. Watch for contagion into cloud/SaaS names.
rec: No action on your current holdings - CRWD is cybersecurity not data center REITs, so direct impact is low. But if cloud sentiment deteriorates more broadly today, it is worth tightening your mental stop on CRWD toward the scanner's 740.31 level.

headline: Hormuz reopening faster than expected - Morgan Stanley cuts oil target
detail: Faster Hormuz reopening and falling oil prices reduce one macro tail risk - energy cost pressure on tech companies and data centers eases. This is a mild background positive for your chip and cloud names since energy is a major operating cost for AI infrastructure. Not a trading catalyst but a slight sentiment tailwind.
rec: No trade warranted - this is a soft macro positive for your book but not specific enough to any holding to justify a move. Just a small reason fewer macro fears are stacking up against tech today.
